what_it_does: The root React component for the Virtual Hub viewer kit. It defines the HUBS list (entry/apt-a/apt-b/rooftop with labels and titles), manages all UI state (active hub, menu open, which panel is showing, and any in-progress capture), and composes the child components (HubBug, MenuButton, QuickMenu, InfoPanel, SharePanel, FloorplanOverlay, CaptureComment). It handles stage clicks to drop a capture comment only when the click lands on the photo background, and renders an onboarding hint when nothing is open. It mounts itself to #app via ReactDOM.createRoot.
observations: HUBS array of four locations, each {id, label, title}; default active is 'apt-a'
Four state atoms: active, menuOpen, panel ('info'|'share'|'floorplan'|null), capture ({x,y}|null)

handleStageClick gates capture creation by checking e.target classList contains 'vh-stage' or 'vh-vignette' (so overlay clicks don't drop comments) and computes click coords relative to the stage via getBoundingClientRect
Panels are mutually exclusive with the menu: info/share only render when !menuOpen
FloorplanOverlay receives activeHub mapped: apt-a becomes 'living', otherwise the raw active id; onPick switches the panel to 'info'
Switching hubs clears any in-progress capture (setCapture(null))
Onboarding hint is an inline-styled pill shown only when !menuOpen && !panel && !capture, with pointerEvents none
Child components (HubBug, MenuButton, QuickMenu, InfoPanel, SharePanel, FloorplanOverlay, CaptureComment) are referenced as globals, not imported — relies on window registration + script load order
Mounts with a DOM guard: only createRoot if #app exists
standouts: Click-target gating so comments drop only on the photo, never on UI chrome — clean separation of canvas vs overlay interaction
Single root holding all viewer state and orchestrating mutually-exclusive overlays
DOM-guarded self-mount (avoids poisoning a shared bundle, per the project's known trap)
conventions: Follows the global-component + script-load-order kit pattern (children are window globals)
DOM-guarded createRoot matches the project CLAUDE.md trap guidance for self-mounting jsx
Uses var() tokens in the inline onboarding-hint style but also raw rgba literals
concerns: Hardcoded HUBS data and the apt-a→'living' floorplan mapping are mockup-specific magic with no data source
Onboarding hint carries inline raw rgba literals rather than tokens/classes
Relies entirely on implicit script load order for its child globals — fragile if order changes (acknowledged kit pattern)
notes: The orchestrator of the whole Virtual Hub viewer: owns hub/menu/panel/capture state and gates photo-clicks into capture comments.
questions: Where do real hub data and panorama images come from in production (HUBS is hardcoded)?
Why does apt-a specifically map to the 'living' floorplan position — is that a per-apartment mapping that would need real data?
purpose_vs_actual: Header 'Virtual Hub viewer root' matches: it is the state-owning root that composes the viewer.
apparent_themes: Single-root state orchestration of mutually-exclusive overlays
intention_signals: Aims to wire the Virtual Hub kit into one interactive whole — switch between property hubs, toggle a menu and panels, navigate by floorplan, and annotate the photo with capture comments.
novelty: The dual interaction model where the same stage both navigates (via chrome) and accepts spatial annotations (via background clicks), arbitrated by click-target class.
contribution: Contributes the orchestration pattern for an immersive media viewer: one root owning hub/menu/panel/capture state, mutually-exclusive overlay arbitration, and photo-vs-chrome click gating that a fused system could reuse for any annotatable media-viewer surface.
summary_for_embedding: The root React component of a Virtual Hub property viewer that owns active-hub/menu/panel/capture state, composes brand-bug/menu/panels/floorplan/capture children, and gates photo-background clicks into spatial capture comments.
intention_for_embedding: Built to wire the Virtual Hub kit into one interactive immersive viewer where a buyer switches hubs, opens panels, navigates by floorplan, and drops capture comments on the photo.
